commands.review_summary:
The PR reroutes denied async exec approval followups with a main session through the agent session followup path before using direct external fallback, and updates focused followup/gateway regression tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +25. Total +34 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, but not by a live run in this review. Current main routes denied async followups away from the session-resume path, and the linked issue plus PR tests describe the strictInlineEval timeout path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer review and real behavior proof rather than an automated repair, because no concrete code defect was found in the patch.

target.title: [codex] Route denied exec approval followups to sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Routes async `Exec denied (...)` approval followups through the originating main agent session before using direct external fallback.
- Keeps strict inline-eval fail-closed behavior: timeout denials still do not run the command.
- Preserves denied-followup suppression for subagent, cron, and no-session cases.
- Adds regression coverage for the gateway non-inline/followup strict inline-eval timeout path so the denial carries the originating `sessionKey` instead of disappearing behind generic transcript repair.

Out of scope: no live channel auth, no Telegram/Discord/VPS validation, no change to approval semantics for successful async exec completions.

## Linked context

Closes #88167

Related #88168 reports a similar placeholder symptom from a different parallel-tool-call path; this PR does not change that path.

Requested in maintainer workflow as `issue-88167`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Strict inline-eval approval-timeout denials on the gateway async followup path now reach the originating main session as an explicit denied followup instead of vanishing and leaving transcript repair to synthesize a generic missing-tool-result placeholder.
- Real environment tested: Local Windows source worktree at `C:\oc-work\oc-88167`, Node `v24.13.0`, repo Vitest runner. No live Telegram, Discord, VPS, or channel auth used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Focused regression `routes denied followups through the originating main session` asserts the same-session agent followup message contains `Exec denied (gateway id=req-denied-main, user-denied): uname -a` and does not contain `missing tool result` or `transcript repair`. Gateway regression `denies timed-out inline-eval requests instead of auto-running them` asserts the async denial is sent with `sessionKey: "agent:main:main"` and exact text `Exec denied (gateway id=req-1, approval-timeout): python3 -c 'print(1)'`.
- Observed result after fix: All focused tests passed: 46/46 in the followup+gateway run, 73/73 in the requested exec-host run, and 71/71 in the requested transcript repair/guard run. `git diff --check` produced no output. Autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
- What was not tested: Live approval timeout through a real Gateway/channel session, by instruction not to use live Telegram/Discord/VPS or live channel auth.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The regression is mocked source-level proof of the routing contract; maintainers may still want a non-secret local Gateway/webchat smoke before merge if the external proof gate requires live runtime evidence.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing tests expected denied async strict inline-eval timeouts to call `sendExecApprovalFollowupResult` with a `null` followup target, matching the lost same-session signal reported in #88167.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```powershell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
git diff --check
python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local
```

Regression coverage added/updated:

- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts` now covers denied main-session followups, session-resume failure fallback, and suppression for subagent/no-session cases.
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts` now pins gateway async strict inline-eval timeout denial to a same-session followup target.

What failed before this fix: The main-session denied followup test would have failed because denied followups returned `false` without calling the agent session path; the gateway strict-inline timeout regression previously expected a `null` followup target.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: Exec approval denial routing.

Mitigation: The denial still fail-closes and never executes the command; subagent/cron/no-session denied followups remain suppressed; direct external denied copy is retained only as a fallback when session resume fails.

## Current review state

Next action: Maintainer review and CI.

Still waiting on: Optional maintainer live/local Gateway proof if required; no live auth was used in this fix.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none.
